email_guide.heading: Forward this email to the client
instructions:
click **Forward** and paste in these email addresses...

{email_recipients}
click **the little arrow thingy** then **edit Subject**
Remove the **Fwd: prefix** from the Subject
Remove everything above and including the **yellow box**.
**Review** the email below, making any minor adjustments necessary.
Check off the checkitem on Trello
**PLEASE REMOVE THIS BOX AND EVERYTHING ABOVE BEFORE SENDING**

member_bios.name: Kassia Zinn
member_bios.title: President, Principal Design Lead
member_bios.bio: Kassia is the cofounder and President of ZINN, and not just on paper. She is the solid center of our office—the one everyone asks for advice on everything from design theory and the psychology of color to tips on how to craft the perfect business email. With her dedicated persistence, she is unruffled by the sometimes messy business of turning lines on paper into three-dimensional structures,  The consummate diplomat, Kassia excels at guiding clients through the complex and sometimes emotional process of designing their home or office. She chooses to build bridges, and easily collaborates with contractors and other project teammates. Kassia earned an undergraduate degree in Architecture with a minor in Women’s Studies and Psychology from the University of Florida. She then completed her graduate studies at Parsons School of Design in New York, where she was awarded the prestigious Michael Kalil Fellowship Grant for her research on disaster site utilization and the role of destruction itself in the creation of space.

member_bios.name: Rob Zinn
member_bios.title: Vice President, Principal in Charge
member_bios.bio: Rob is the cofounder and Vice President of ZINN. He is a creative and curious multipotentialite, so nerding out over everything from flashing details to how cities could work better comes naturally to him. Rob’s operating principle is that architects should balance their design expertise with technical  know-how to create solutions that are not just beautiful, but fuctional, logical, cost-effective. Rob earned his master’s degree in Architecture from the University of Florida, where he met Kassia. He earned his BA from Boston University for, you guessed it, pre-med with a double major in English Literature and Art History. Rob serves as the Vice Chair of the Northeast Florida Builder’s Association Commercial Council. His work with NEFBA encourages dense walkable commercial development and improved relationships between architects, builders, and developers.

member_bios.name: Hannah Jensen
member_bios.title: Interior Designer I
member_bios.bio: hannah jensen    Hannah is our Interior Designer 1, a graduate of Florida State College at Jacksonville with a degree in Interior Design Technology. Her passion for design was ignited as a young child where she would sketch perspectives of her room during church. This passion grew over time and cultivated into a love for Mid-Century Modern and Bauhaus design. During her off days, she loves scouring antique markets and vintage shops for unique vintage pieces. She also loves watching movies, buying records and participating in the local music scene. Hannah’s approach to design is taking the time to get to know the people behind the project, and to curate a space that best reflects each client’s personality in the most aesthetically pleasing and functional way possible. Her design inspirations include Kelly Wearstler, Verner Panton, Charles and Ray Eames, and Mies Van der Rohe.

member_bios.name: Robin Tuazon
member_bios.title: Project Architect
member_bios.bio: Leveraging her background in creating modern, light-filled homes as an Architect in her tropical home country where she ran her own practice, Robin seeks to apply her design and project management skills to a similar environment, finding resonance in Florida’s ambiance. With a keen ability to read people and a knack for crafting designs that scream ‘life’s a beach,’ Robin’s creations are not just buildings—they’re experiences. Known for her positive and calm demeanor, she approaches the dynamic architecture industry with a solutions-oriented mindset. Outside the studio, you’ll find her embracing the serenity of yoga, and frolicking along the sandy shores. She’s always up for trying new activities, whether it’s conquering a wave on a surfboard, carving fresh powder on the slopes, or simply obsessively planning packed itineraries for new travels. Fueled by exploration, Robin’s designs are always one adventure ahead.

member_bios.name: Daniel Paul
member_bios.title: Architectural Designer
member_bios.bio: Daniel is a determined architectural designer on a pathway to a brighter and more sustainable future with eight years in the field drafting and designing. A graduate from Jefferson University’s College of Architecture & the Built Environment in Philadelphia, he has always explored how buildings interact and exist in the greater surrounding landscape, inspired at an early age by several visits to Fallingwater. He is a firm believer that for a project to be successful a client needs to experience their projects emotionally - parametric & unique materials use drive these designs, fueled by architects such as Kengo Kuma & Coop Himmelb(l)au. His passions outside the office include photography, fashion, automotive design, and landscape architecture. When he isn’t working or traveling, he loves to guest juror for current architecture students and mentor through the ACE Program, in hopes that he can bring more designers out of the constraints of modern stereotypical design.

member_bios.name: Abby Labial
member_bios.title: Architecture Intern
member_bios.bio: Abby is an M.Architecture student currently studying architecture & community design at the University of South Florida. Her passion for design began when she was younger, spending countless hours on Minecraft building digital worlds in interactive 3D environments. Over time, she became inspired by the works of Frank Gehry and Zaha Hadid, which grew her love of experimenting with organic structures and parametric shapes. This piqued her interest in how unique, unconventional design solutions relate to the built environment. Outside of the studio, Abby enjoys indulging in her creative endeavors that include art, design, writing, and music. She also enjoys exploring, traveling, making jewelry, and woodworking on the weekends. Fueled by her ambition, curiosity, and tenacity, she isn’t afraid to try something new.

member_bios.name: Ryan Wills
member_bios.title: Business Development Representative
member_bios.bio: Ryan is a Business Development Representative with a natural ability to connect with people and build meaningful relationships. A graduate of Nease High School and current student at Florida Atlantic University, he thrives on creating opportunities through conversation and helping clients find solutions that fit their needs. Whether attending networking events, meeting new people, or collaborating with colleagues, Ryan enjoys the process of turning introductions into lasting professional connections. He believes that strong relationships are at the center of every successful business and approaches each interaction with energy, curiosity, and authenticity. Outside of work, Ryan enjoys expanding his network, staying active, and spending time on the golf course. He is always looking for opportunities to learn, grow, and connect with others both professionally and personally.
